narrative.headline: 2022 GMC Acadia: electrical and engine issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 45 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the single biggest concern at 31% of reports, with the engine close behind at 29%. The fuel/propulsion system follows at 16%, then power train and lane departure at 9% each. None of these reports involved a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The top complaint area: 14 reports (31%). No crashes, fires, or injuries were reported, but check that electronics and driver-assist features work reliably.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 13 reports (29%). No crashes, fires, or injuries were reported. Listen for any unusual engine behavior on a test drive.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 7 reports (16%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Confirm the engine light is off and there are no pending fuel-system fault codes.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 4 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Test how the car drives and shifts under load.

issues.title: Lane Departure
issues.blurb: 4 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Check that lane-departure and cruise features behave as expected on a test drive.
